message:
fix(runtime): correctness fixes across scheduler, budget, region, and transport

A sweep of correctness and semantic alignment fixes across multiple subsystems:

- cx/scope.rs: Correct misleading comment in race() — pinned h2 is NOT dropped
  when h1 wins (it's borrowed by Select); explicit abort_with_reason() is
  required to prevent deadlock.

- net/tcp/split.rs: Suppress false-positive clippy::significant_drop_tightening
  lint on register_interest() — lock is intentionally held across register()
  to prevent ADD race condition.

- record/region.rs: Add early-return guard in heap_alloc() rejecting allocations
  when region is in terminal state. Change error variant from AllocationInvalid
  to RegionClosed in rref_get/rref_get_mut for more precise error reporting.

- runtime/builder.rs: Change RuntimeInner task spawning from Budget::INFINITE
  (priority 255) to Budget::new() (priority 128) so spawned tasks use standard
  default priority instead of maximum.

- runtime/scheduler/three_lane.rs: Add with_task_table() accessor and implement
  lazy worker pinning in inject_ready and wake paths — when a task is local to
  a worker but lacks explicit pin_to_worker assignment, pin it to the current
  worker so subsequent cross-thread wakes/cancels route correctly.

- types/budget.rs: Align Budget semantics — INFINITE.priority now 255 (truly
  maximal), new() returns priority 128 (distinct from INFINITE), meet() uses
  min instead of max for priority merging (most-constrained intersection).

- remote.rs, transport/aggregator.rs, transport/router.rs: Transport layer
  improvements for routing and aggregation logic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
